name: American United
description_short: American United Federal Credit Union is a Utah-based credit union offering personal and business checking, savings, loans, credit cards, and wealth management services to members.
description_long:
American United Federal Credit Union operates as a member-owned, not-for-profit financial institution serving Utah residents and businesses. As a federal credit union, it is NCUA-insured and regulated as a credit union rather than a traditional bank. The organization has maintained a significant online presence at amucu.org with both personal and business banking divisions.

The credit union provides a comprehensive suite of financial products including personal and business checking accounts (Primary Checking, GoldStar Membership, ATM Checking, Fresh Start Checking), savings vehicles (Primary Savings, Youth Savings, Club Accounts, Money Market, Certificates, IRAs), vehicle financing (auto loans and recreational loans), home lending (mortgage loans and home equity lines of credit), personal loans, and VISA credit card options with rewards or cash back features. Additionally, they offer money services such as wire transfers, cashier's checks, money orders, notary services, and safe deposit boxes. Their insurance and wealth management division includes identity theft protection, life insurance, auto and property insurance, and financial advisor services.

American United distinguishes itself through member-focused programs including a referral bonus program, end-of-year dividends, monthly dividends, youth financial education (MoneyEdu), and specialized offerings like skip-a-payment loan protections and guaranteed asset protection on vehicle loans. They provide free financial checkups, maintain a financial wellness center with learning courses available in English and Spanish, and offer discounted community partnerships (Lagoon tickets, CARFAX reports). The CO-OP Shared Banking network extends their service availability beyond their physical branch locations.

As a credit union, American United operates under the not-for-profit model where members share in profits through dividends rather than external shareholders extracting value. However, the website content provided does not include specific information about membership eligibility requirements, current APR rates for most products (except credit cards starting at 9.90%), fee structures, or comparative advantage data. Prospective members should verify membership eligibility and compare rates and fees with other local financial institutions.
services: Personal checking accounts (Primary, GoldStar, ATM, Fresh Start)
Business checking accounts (Basic, Premier with rewards debit card)
Savings accounts (Primary, Youth, Club, Money Market, Certificates)
Individual Retirement Accounts (IRAs) and IRA Certificates
Auto loans and recreational vehicle loans
Mortgage loans and home equity lines of credit
Personal signature loans and holiday loans
VISA credit cards (Rewards, Cash Back, Secured)
Commercial real estate and business equipment financing
Wire transfers, cashier's checks, money orders, notary services
Identity theft protection, life insurance, auto insurance, property insurance
Financial advisor services and retirement planning
pros: Member-owned, not-for-profit credit union structure returns profits to members via dividends
NCUA insurance protection on deposits up to $250,000
Comprehensive product range including checking, savings, loans, credit cards, mortgages, and insurance
Specialized loan protections including skip-a-payment options and guaranteed asset protection (GAP)
Free financial wellness resources including MoneyEdu courses and free financial checkups
Youth financial education programs and youth-specific savings accounts with grade rewards
Access to CO-OP Shared Banking network for expanded ATM and branch access beyond Utah locations
cons: Website does not display current APR rates for most loan products, only credit cards listed at 9.90% minimum
No information about membership eligibility requirements or who can join
Limited transparency on fee structures for checking accounts, transfers, or other services
Geographic limitation as Utah-based credit union with no indication of online-only membership options
Website content does not clarify lending requirements, credit score minimums, or approval timelines
best_for: Utah residents seeking a member-owned alternative to traditional banks with dividend participation
Families wanting youth financial education and children's savings products with rewards
Vehicle buyers interested in auto loans with skip-a-payment protection and GAP coverage
Small business owners seeking SBA lending (7a and 504 loans) and business credit products
diagnosis: American United Federal Credit Union is best for Utah-based individuals and small business owners seeking member-owned banking with dividend participation and comprehensive loan/investment services. The main caveat is the lack of publicly displayed rate and fee information on their website, requiring direct contact with the credit union to compare their specific terms against competitors.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: American United — West Jordan, UT Credit Union | CreditDoc
meta_description: American United — NCUA-insured credit union in West Jordan, UT. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
